message: Let’s try to trade every round. My value isn’t very high, so if you keep asks in the low 40s we can both earn steady profit over time. What’s the lowest ask you’re comfortable with?
Lowest comfortable ask is 52 right now. If you can bid around there consistently, we should be able to trade regularly and both earn something.
Nice, that trade worked. My value’s only mid‑50s, so I need decent margin. How about you ask 50, I bid 52?
Glad it worked. Since 52 cleared, let’s keep it simple: I’ll ask 52 again if you bid 52 or 53. That should keep trades reliable.
To keep this worthwhile on my side, I need to move up slightly. If you bid 53 today, I’ll ask 53 so we can keep trading reliably and still leave you some margin.
My value’s only mid‑50s, so 53 leaves too little margin over many rounds. I’ll keep bidding 52; please keep asking 52 so we both earn steady profit. If asks rise, I may need to skip trades.
We’ve had three clean trades; to continue, I need 53 today. If you bid 53, I’ll ask 53 and we can keep things reliable.
At 53 my margin is tiny over many rounds. My value’s only around low‑50s, so 52 is my ceiling for now. If you keep asking 52, I’ll keep bidding 52 every round and we both lock in
I’ll keep 52 today if you keep bidding 52. Reliable trades are best for both of us; if bids change later, I’ll reassess my ask.
Deal, I’ll keep bidding 52 this round and going forward as long as you keep your ask at 52. Stable trades at this level give us both steady profit over the whole game.
I’ll ask 52 again today, and as long as you keep bidding 52, we can keep trading steadily.
Thanks, I’ll keep bidding 52 this round. Looking ahead, my margin is getting tight; from around round 8 I’ll probably need your ask at 51 to keep trading every round instead of skipping occasionally.
I’ll stick with bid 52 this round to keep our trade. But starting next round, I’ll only bid 51 if your ask stays at 52; my margin’s too thin otherwise, so I’ll skip trades when price
Let's keep 52 for now. We've both profited steadily there. If you drop to 51, trade stops and both lose. Hold 52 a few more rounds, then we can revisit later.
We’ve both earned well at 52, so I’ll keep bidding 52 through round 10. From round 11 on, I’d need your ask at 51; otherwise I’ll occasionally skip trades to protect my margin.
Appreciate it. Let’s keep 52 through round 12, then I’ll seriously consider 51 for the final stretch. Stable trades now maximize both our totals and avoid unnecessary missed rounds.
Let’s keep the reliable 52/52 arrangement today and through round 12. We’re both earning steadily, and missed trades hurt both of us more than holding this price a few rounds longer.

Agreed, I’ll keep bidding 52 through round 12 so we both lock in steady profit. From round 13 onward, I’ll only be comfortable bidding if your ask drops to 51; otherwise I may skip trades to protect margin.
We’ve both done well at 52. Let’s keep 52/52 through round 14, then revisit for the final stretch to avoid unnecessary missed trades now.
I’ll hold to my word: I’ll keep bidding 52 through round 12. From round 13 on, I’ll only bid if your ask is 51; otherwise I’ll likely skip trades, which just leaves surplus on the table.
Let's keep 52 through the finish. Skipping rounds only destroys surplus, and 52 has worked reliably for ten trades. If you keep bidding 52, I'll keep asking 52 and we both keep earning.
I’ll honor what I said: I’ll keep bidding 52 for rounds 11–12. From round 13 on, I’ll only be comfortable bidding if your ask is 51; above that I may skip trades to protect my margin.
This round I’ll still bid 52 as promised. But from round 13 on, I won’t bid 52 anymore—only 51 or I may skip. If you drop your ask to 51, we can keep trading every remaining round.
I’ll ask 52 in round 13; if you keep bidding 52 there, I’ll seriously consider 51 after that. Cutting immediately just risks a needless missed trade.
I’ll move to 51 starting this round if you bid 51 consistently through round 20. Let’s lock in every remaining trade and avoid unnecessary misses.
Deal—I’ll bid 51 from this round through round 20. If you keep your ask at 51 so we trade every remaining round, we both lock in steady profit with no wasted rounds.
I’ll keep asking 51 through round 20 if you keep bidding 51 each round. Let’s lock in every remaining trade with no surprises or missed rounds.
Agreed—I’ll keep bidding 51 every round through 20 as promised. No surprises or changes from my side; let’s keep locking in these trades at 51 so we both earn steadily to the end.
Agreed—I’ll keep asking 51 this round and through round 20 as long as you keep bidding 51. Let’s finish with guaranteed trades every round and no last-minute changes.
Sounds good—I’ll keep bidding 51 each round through 20 as promised so we secure every remaining trade without risk of missed rounds or last-minute changes.
I’ll keep asking 51 through round 20 if you keep bidding 51. Let’s finish with certain trades and no surprises.
I’ll stick with bidding 51 every round through 20 as promised so we keep locking in guaranteed trades without any last-minute changes.
I’ll ask 51 again this round. Let’s keep the trade certain and check in again after today for the finish.
Perfect, I’ll keep bidding 51 this round and through round 20 so we guarantee trades every remaining round with no changes. Let’s just hold 51/51 to the end and avoid any risk of missed payoffs.
I’ll keep asking 51 through round 20 as agreed if you keep bidding 51. Let’s finish with certain trades and no surprises in these last three rounds.
I’m happy to keep bidding 51 through round 20 as promised. If you’re comfortable dropping your ask to 50 in the last round or two, I’ll still bid 51; if not, I’m fine locking in 51/
I’ll ask 51 this round. For the last round, I’ll likely return to 52; if you bid 52 then, we should still close with a final profitable trade.
I’ll bid 51 this round as agreed. For the final round, I won’t bid above 51—if your ask is 52, I’ll likely skip. Keeping your ask at 51 guarantees we both earn on both remaining rounds.
Last round I’ll still only bid 51, as promised—I won’t bid 52. If you keep your ask at 51, we both lock in one more certain, profitable trade instead of risking zero.
I’ll keep my ask at 51 for the final round. Let’s close with one certain trade and finish cleanly.
